name: Brazil
topIncomeNote: Top rate of the federal personal income tax schedule (PwC headline PIT rate).
corporateNote: Combined 34%: corporate income tax (IRPJ) at 25% (15% plus 10% surtax) plus social contribution on net profits (CSLL) at 9% (PwC).
vatNote: No single national VAT. Per PwC: state VAT (ICMS) normally 17-20%, federal PIS/COFINS combined 3.65% (cumulative) or 9.25% (non-cumulative), federal excise (IPI) typically 5-30%, municipal service tax (ISS) 2-5%.
taxToGdpNote: OECD Revenue Statistics in Latin America and the Caribbean 2026 country note; general government, includes social security contributions.

sources.label: PwC Worldwide Tax Summaries - Brazil
OECD Revenue Statistics in LAC 2026 - Brazil

name: Russia
topIncomeNote: Five-band progressive scale (13/15/18/20/22%) introduced 1 January 2025; the 22% top rate applies to annual income above RUB 50 million. PwC no longer publishes a Russia summary, so figures are from legal advisories covering the 2024 tax reform law.
corporateNote: General corporate income tax rate raised from 20% to 25% from 1 January 2025.
vatNote: Standard VAT rate increased from 20% to 22% under Federal Law No. 425-FZ (adopted 28 November 2025), applying from 2026; a 10% preferential rate is retained for some products.
taxToGdpNote: World Bank series 'Tax revenue (% of GDP)' (GC.TAX.TOTL.GD.ZS), central government only - excludes sub-national taxes and social contributions, so general-government burden is higher.

sources.label: Forte Tax & Law - Tax changes in Russia from 2025
Konsu - Changes to the Russian Tax Code 2026 (VAT to 22%)
World Bank - Tax revenue (% of GDP), Russia

name: India
topIncomeNote: PwC headline maximum marginal rate under the default new personal tax regime: 30% top slab plus 25% surcharge plus 4% health and education cess = 39%.
corporateNote: Domestic companies: base rate 25% or 30% depending on turnover, plus applicable surcharge and cess; concessional 22% or 15% regimes available subject to conditions (PwC).
vatNote: GST standard rate 18%; GST has multiple slabs ranging from 5% to 28% depending on the goods or services supplied (PwC).
taxToGdpNote: World Bank series 'Tax revenue (% of GDP)' (GC.TAX.TOTL.GD.ZS), central government only - excludes state taxes, so the general-government ratio is substantially higher. 2022 is the most recent non-empty value.

sources.label: PwC Worldwide Tax Summaries - India
World Bank - Tax revenue (% of GDP), India

name: China
topIncomeNote: Top rate of the progressive individual income tax on comprehensive income (PwC headline PIT rate).
corporateNote: Standard enterprise income tax rate (PwC headline CIT rate).
vatNote: Standard VAT rate 13% for most goods; reduced rates of 9% and 6% apply depending on the goods or services (PwC).
taxToGdpNote: OECD Revenue Statistics in Asia and the Pacific 2025 country note; general government, includes social security contributions.

sources.label: PwC Worldwide Tax Summaries - China
OECD Revenue Statistics in Asia and the Pacific 2025 - China

name: South Africa
topIncomeNote: Top marginal personal income tax rate (PwC headline PIT rate).
corporateNote: Standard corporate income tax rate (PwC headline CIT rate).
taxToGdpNote: OECD Revenue Statistics in Africa 2024 country factsheet; general government.

sources.label: PwC Worldwide Tax Summaries - South Africa
OECD Revenue Statistics in Africa 2024 - South Africa

name: United Arab Emirates
topIncomeNote: No personal income tax; PwC lists the headline PIT rate as 'NA' (the territory does not have the tax).
corporateNote: New federal corporate income tax with a 9% headline rate (PwC headline CIT rate).
taxToGdpNote: World Bank series 'Tax revenue (% of GDP)' (GC.TAX.TOTL.GD.ZS), central government only; most UAE government revenue comes from sources outside this series.

sources.label: PwC Worldwide Tax Summaries - United Arab Emirates
World Bank - Tax revenue (% of GDP), UAE

name: Saudi Arabia
topIncomeNote: No individual income tax scheme; employment income earned in Saudi Arabia is not taxed. Non-residents' Saudi-source income with no permanent establishment is taxed under withholding tax rules (PwC).
corporateNote: 20% corporate income tax applies to the share of a resident company owned by non-Saudi/non-GCC persons and to non-residents with Saudi-source income; Saudi and GCC nationals' shareholdings pay Zakat at 2.5% of the Zakat base instead. Oil and hydrocarbon production is taxed at 50-85% (PwC).
vatNote: Standard VAT rate 15% since 1 July 2020 (5% before that) (PwC).
taxToGdpNote: World Bank series 'Tax revenue (% of GDP)' (GC.TAX.TOTL.GD.ZS), central government only; excludes oil revenue collected through non-tax channels.

sources.label: PwC Worldwide Tax Summaries - Saudi Arabia
PwC - Saudi Arabia corporate taxes on income
World Bank - Tax revenue (% of GDP), Saudi Arabia

name: Singapore
topIncomeNote: Top marginal rate for resident individuals (PwC headline PIT rate).
corporateNote: Flat corporate income tax rate (PwC headline CIT rate).
vatNote: Goods and services tax (GST) standard rate (PwC).
taxToGdpNote: OECD Revenue Statistics in Asia and the Pacific 2025 country note; general government.

sources.label: PwC Worldwide Tax Summaries - Singapore
OECD Revenue Statistics in Asia and the Pacific 2025 - Singapore

name: Hong Kong
topIncomeNote: Salaries tax: progressive rates run 2-17%, but total tax is capped at the two-tiered standard rates - 15% on the first HKD 5 million of net income and 16% on the remainder (2025/26). Taxpayers pay the lower of the two computations, so 16% is the effective top statutory rate (PwC).
corporateNote: Profits tax: 16.5% for corporations, 15% for unincorporated businesses (PwC headline rates).
vatNote: No VAT/GST (PwC lists the standard VAT rate as 'NA').
taxToGdpNote: OECD Revenue Statistics in Asia and the Pacific 2025 country note for Hong Kong (China); general government.

sources.label: PwC Worldwide Tax Summaries - Hong Kong SAR
PwC - Hong Kong SAR individual taxes on personal income
OECD Revenue Statistics in Asia and the Pacific 2025 - Hong Kong (China)
